  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Mixed old and new belts installed on a multi-groove sheave drive",
    "Unequal load distribution across the belt set",
    "Accelerated wear on the new belts due to carrying the majority of the load"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Shut down the equipment and lock out/tag out",
    "Remove all existing belts from the multi-groove sheave",
    "Inspect sheaves for wear using a groove gauge",
    "Install a brand new, matched set of belts from the same manufacturer",
    "Tension the new belt set to manufacturer specifications"
  ],
  "rationale": "Mixing old and new belts on a multi-groove drive is a critical failure mode. Because older belts have already stretched and worn down in cross-section, they sit deeper in the sheave grooves and carry less load. The new belts are forced to carry the entire load of the drive, leading to rapid slippage, overheating, accelerated wear, and premature drive failure. Belts on multi-groove sheaves must always be replaced as a complete, matched set.",
  "references": [
    "OEM Belt Maintenance and Installation Manuals",
    "Power Transmission Distributors Association (PTDA) Belt Drive Installation Guidelines"
  ]
}
